description: Recto: note to Abū l-Maḥāsin, asking for guidance in a personal problem (probably in relation to the query on verso), which also involves the fact that ‘she brought 33 and a half’. The writer asks for a quick reply. Verso: legal query in the hand of Shelomo b. Elijah to Abraham Maimonides, with the title Nagid (post-1213 CE), concerning a man who lives with his wife in a house together with another man and suspects his wife and the housemate of having an affair, and therefore wants to move out, but the wife is refusing to go along with his plans. (Information from CUDL)
description_he: מסמך משפטי בHebrew, Judaeo-Arabic מהמאה ה-13. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
